comments.author: RebarBotNava
comments.body: Implemented + pushed to Gerrit change #459 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/459). batch_stage_and_commit + batch_write_and_push in _store/event_append.py; all-or-nothing rollback; _recover_from_unmerged generalized to full path list; docs/scale-envelope.md corrected. Local: make lint && make typecheck && make test all green. Do NOT close until Gerrit Verified +1.

compiled_state.description:
## What
Add `batch_stage_and_commit(tracker, items)` (and a batched `write_and_push` variant) to
`src/rebar/_store/event_append.py`: commit MANY events under ONE write-lock acquire + ONE
`git commit`, all-or-nothing. `items` is a sequence of `(ticket_id, event_dict)`.

## Why
The single-event write path (`stage_and_commit`) commits one git graph per event (~3.3
commits/event). The write lock (`_store/lock.py`) is NOT re-entrant, so a caller cannot
loop `stage_and_commit` under one lock. A dedicated batch primitive is the seam bulk
callers (the importer, B2) use to collapse N commits into 1. Batching is invariant-safe
because replay/dedup/union-merge/SNAPSHOT-compaction key off each event's per-event UUID,
not commit boundaries (each event stays its own I2 uuid-named file).

## Scope
- `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py` only. Single-event `stage_and_commit`/`write_and_push`
  behavior unchanged (the reversibility guarantee).
- Validate + canonical-stage every event BEFORE taking the lock; then under ONE lock:
  atomic-rename each temp into `{ticket}/{ts}-{uuid}-{TYPE}.json`, one `git add`, ONE
  `git commit`.
- All-or-nothing rollback: any failure (validation, rename, add, non-recoverable commit)
  unstages AND unlinks ALL already-placed paths, not just the failing one.
- Generalize `_recover_from_unmerged` to re-add the FULL batch path list on retry.
- Empty batch = no-op (no lock, no commit).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `batch_stage_and_commit(tracker, items)` in `src/rebar/_store/event_append.py`: one lock acquire, N I2 uuid-named files, exactly one commit; returns the event count
- [ ] `batch_write_and_push` variant: batch commit then ONE best-effort push
- [ ] All-or-nothing rollback on validation / rename / `git add` / non-recoverable `git commit` failure: every already-renamed path unstaged from the index AND unlinked from disk
- [ ] `_recover_from_unmerged` (bug 6818 UU self-heal) re-adds the full batch path list on retry — a batched commit self-heals a pre-existing unmerged `.bridge_state/*`
- [ ] Empty batch is a no-op (no lock taken, no commit made)
- [ ] Single-event `stage_and_commit`/`write_and_push` path unchanged; existing regression tests (`tests/unit/test_store_write_index_reset_on_failure.py`, `tests/unit/test_store_write_unmerged_recovery.py`) stay green
- [ ] New unit tests in `tests/unit/test_store_batch_write.py` cover: N-in-one-commit, canonical-byte parity vs single path, empty no-op, validation/commit rollback, next-write-uncontaminated, UU self-heal
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` all green
compiled_state.title: B1: batch_stage_and_commit — batched-commit write primitive in _store
